authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69O Internet and Mobile Gaming
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. iGaming regulation covering patron account creation, safeguarded funds, complaint handling, adjustment reviews, system reports, and player-protection controls in an active regulated market.
used_for: complaint routing and monthly or daily review expectations

authoritative_sources.title: RTS 1 Customer account information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official remote gambling technical standard defining the account information, balances, and transaction history that must be visible to customers and operators.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Account-history validation and player-visible transaction support
used_for: closure evidence for account or cash cases

authoritative_sources.title: Interacting with the customer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official formal guidance for remote gambling licensees that defines how operators must respond when indicators of harm appear, making it authoritative for safer-gambling intervention workflow stages.

authoritative_sources.title: Keeping records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official formal guidance requiring operators to preserve interaction evidence, rationale, and evaluation outcomes, which directly constrains what counts as completion-ready evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting customer funds
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official customer-funds guidance defining protection status disclosure and segregation posture, which materially affects wallet and withdrawal handling in remote gambling.

authoritative_sources.title: Directive 2 of 2018 Player Protection Directive
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary MGA remote-gaming directive covering real-time balance visibility, six-month gambling history, withdrawal restrictions, player funds, complaints, and ADR handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions - Casino Recordkeeping, Reporting, and Compliance Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AML guidance for casino recordkeeping and reporting obligations that changes when cash, account, or transaction exceptions must be escalated instead of worked as ordinary operations tickets.

authoritative_sources.title: GLI-19 Interactive Gaming Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interactive-gaming systems standard used across jurisdictions for audit trails, event logs, geolocation, account controls, and transaction integrity expectations.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and jurisdiction prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate player identity context, jurisdiction eligibility, account state, retrieval hooks, and the minimum wallet, session, and player-protection records required before work can proceed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Player-account, wallet, and wager-state review
workflow_stages.description: Work the case through account-history review, wallet and pending-transaction checks, wager or game-round state checks, and manual operational next-step determination using authoritative system evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Session, withdrawal, dispute, and player-service exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move interrupted-session, withdrawal-review, complaint, dispute, or account-adjustment work into advance, hold, rework, or escalated states without silently bypassing required controls.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Safer-gambling, AML, fraud, and restricted-account escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop normal processing and route the work when self-exclusion, player-harm indicators, suspicious activity, fraud, or final compliance interpretation changes whether the case may continue.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and audit-ready evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close work only when wallet, processor, session, complaint, interaction, and review records support completion and the named systems of record agree on final state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: player account, KYC, identity, age, jurisdiction, and restriction records
wallet ledger, deposit, withdrawal, pending-transaction, payment-processor, and adjustment reports
wager, game-round, session, interruption, settlement, and account-history records
geolocation, device, IP, and session-risk evidence
bonus, promotion, terms-and-conditions, and player-notification artifacts
responsible-gambling interactions, self-exclusion records, limit settings, complaint logs, and ADR records
AML, suspicious-activity, fraud-review, source-of-funds, and account-review artifacts
execution status updates, exception summaries, completion records, and escalation notes

systems_of_record.name: player account management platform
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for registration, account state, limits, self-exclusion, identity status, and player entitlement records.

systems_of_record.name: wallet and payment orchestration system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for balances, deposits, withdrawals, pending transactions, and account adjustments.

systems_of_record.name: game transaction and wagering event log
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for wager placement, game rounds, interruptions, settlements, voids, and outcome history.

systems_of_record.name: geolocation and device-risk platform
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for jurisdiction checks, device fingerprinting, session eligibility, and location-sensitive blockers.

systems_of_record.name: CRM or player-service case platform
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for complaint intake, player contacts, dispute workflow state, and owner routing.

systems_of_record.name: responsible-gambling and compliance case system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for safer-gambling interactions, self-exclusion escalations, AML or fraud referrals, and regulator-sensitive review notes.

systems_of_record.name: document repository and audit log
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative archive for terms, player notices, processor confirmations, interaction records, and manual review evidence.

allowed: Advance, hold, rework, or complete operational work based on documented account, wallet, session, and player-service evidence.
Confirm whether required records exist and support lifecycle progression or closure.
Prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, exception summaries, and escalations using iGaming-specific artifacts and terminology.
escalate: Escalate when self-exclusion, responsible-gambling, AML, fraud, or account-takeover signals change whether the work may proceed.
Escalate when processor, wallet, PAM, geolocation, or wager logs materially disagree on account state or funds status.
Escalate when public guidance, jurisdiction rules, or tenant policy materially conflict on withdrawal, complaint, or player-funds handling.
Escalate when final KYC, compliance, legal, or payment-risk authority is required.
refuse: Refuse requests to reopen or clear a restricted account without authoritative approval and evidence.
Refuse requests to alter wallet balances, wager outcomes, bonus confiscations, or adjustment history without traceable source support.
Refuse requests to close a case while settlement, complaint disposition, or interaction evidence is still missing.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Processing cash-out or account work without current jurisdiction or geolocation truth
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: iGaming activity is location-sensitive and remote account handling cannot be treated as generic support work when jurisdiction eligibility controls the outcome.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Treating wallet balance or pending-transaction status as final when processor, ledger, or game-round evidence disagrees
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Remote gaming combines player wallet, processor, and wager systems whose disagreement can change player-funds status and regulator exposure.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Resolving self-exclusion, safer-gambling, or high-risk interaction cases as normal service tickets
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Player-protection controls are regulated operational duties, not optional customer-service choices.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Applying unsupported account adjustments, bonus confiscations, or withdrawal restrictions for operational convenience
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: iGaming operations must preserve audit trails, approved terms, and player-funds controls rather than improvising financial changes.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Closing complaints or disputes without recorded rationale, player-contact history, and escalation evidence
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Remote-gaming complaint handling, ADR routing, and interaction records are explicit control obligations rather than generic case notes.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific thresholds and procedural rules for age checks, source-of-funds review, withdrawal restrictions, and regulator reporting
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules vary materially by jurisdiction, operator license, and tenant policy, so the pack records them as required runtime retrieval inputs instead of guessing.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The public regulator and standards baseline is sufficient to define safe workflow stages, evidence posture, refusal triggers, and escalation boundaries for a validated spec pack.
